quick_skim.title: All-sky modeling of Galactic emission at radio and microwave frequencies
quick_skim.abstract: We present a new all-sky model of low-frequency diffuse Galactic emission in the regime where synchrotron, free-free, and spinning dust dominate. The model extends the Planck 2015 diffuse component-separation analysis by incorporating recent radio and microwave surveys. We fit 35 full- and partial-sky maps at 1 degree resolution, including S-PASS at 2.30 GHz, C-BASS at 4.76 GHz, and QUIJOTE at 10-20 GHz, together with reprocessed WMAP and Planck LFI data from the Cosmoglobe collaboration and Planck HFI channels. Using a Bayesian parametric approach with Commander, we derive spatially varying amplitude and spectral parameter maps for the dominant low-frequency foreground components in total intensity. The main products are a full-sky synchrotron amplitude and spectral-index solution, an all-sky characterization of spinning dust emission with a single-component log-normal spectral model, and a reconstructed all-sky total-intensity map at 4.76 GHz tracing diffuse synchrotron emission with reduced systematics relative to Haslam 408 MHz. The revised low-frequency anchoring increases the recovered synchrotron amplitude: at 4.76 GHz, it is approximately a factor of two higher than the Planck 2015 prediction. The model achieves RMS temperature residuals below 10 $\mathrmμ$K over 95% of the sky up to 353 GHz, with fractional residuals below 1.5% in the Galactic plane and below 5% across QUIJOTE bands. Residual angular power spectra lie more than two orders of magnitude below the CMB spectrum. These products describe the transition between radio and microwave emission and provide a new reference for foreground modeling and sky-simulation applications.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: provides all-sky foreground model crucial for pulsar timing observations at low frequencies
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提供全天空前景模型，对低频脉冲星计时观测至关重要
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提供全天空前景模型，对低频脉冲星计时观测至关重要
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提供全天空前景模型，对低频脉冲星计时观测至关重要
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Presents a new all-sky model of diffuse Galactic emission at radio frequencies, enabling accurate foreground subtraction for pulsar observations.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文构建了一个新的全天空低频银河弥漫射电模型，涵盖同步辐射、自由-自由和旋转尘埃发射。利用Commander贝叶斯方法，拟合了多个射电和微波巡天数据，得到空间变化的振幅和谱指数图。该模型对于从银河系前景中分离脉冲星信号、提高脉冲星计时精度具有重要应用价值，是脉冲星观测的一项关键工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文构建了一个新的全天空低频银河弥漫射电模型，涵盖同步辐射、自由-自由和旋转尘埃发射。利用Commander贝叶斯方法，拟合了多个射电和微波巡天数据，得到空间变化的振幅和谱指数图。该模型对于从银河系前景中分离脉冲星信号、提高脉冲星计时精度具有重要应用价值，是脉冲星观测的一项关键工具。

quick_skim.title: Nuclear Isomers and Their Impact on Gamma-Ray Emission in Binary Neutron Star Mergers
quick_skim.abstract: The multi-messenger observations of GW170817 have provided strong evidence that binary neutron star mergers are a site of heavy-element production through the rapid neutron capture process. The decay of unstable $r$-process nuclei produces a significant number of $γ$-rays, which not only power the associated kilonova, but may also be observable in current and future $γ$-ray observatories, providing a direct probe of the nuclei synthesized. Current models of the emitted $γ$-rays link a nuclear reaction network with individual decay spectra. Typically, only the population of the ground state of the nuclei is tracked in the reaction network, and rapid de-excitation of the daughter nuclei is assumed when calculating the decay spectra. This may not be accurate in nuclei where there are longer-lived, high-energy nuclear isomers. In this work, we relax these assumptions, modeling the dynamic behavior of nuclear isomers for a select list of nuclei. These isomers are included as independent states in the network, with temperature-dependent effective transition rates between ground and isomeric states and $β$-feeding probabilities calculated using the nuclear level structure. We estimate the $γ$-ray flux from isomeric transitions, finding that the 743.3 keV line of Nb-97m and the 555.6 keV line of Y-91m may be detectable in $γ$-ray observatories such as COSI, AMEGO, and LOX at galactic distances. These results, calculated for a small fraction of nuclei with isomers, highlight the need for the robust inclusion of nuclear isomers in nuclear reaction networks, as well as careful modeling of the resultant $γ$-ray spectroscopy to characterize observational signals.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: r-process nucleosynthesis in neutron star mergers, impacting equation of state constraints
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 双中子星并合中的r过程核合成，影响状态方程约束

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Studies the effect of nuclear isomers on gamma-ray emission from binary neutron star mergers, providing direct probes of r-process nucleosynthesis and linking to equation of state models.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文研究了核同质异能素对双中子星并合伽马射线发射的影响。当前模型仅追踪基态，而激发态的延迟退激会改变伽马射线谱，从而影响对并合产物和状态方程的推断。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文研究了核同质异能素对双中子星并合伽马射线发射的影响。当前模型仅追踪基态，而激发态的延迟退激会改变伽马射线谱，从而影响对并合产物和状态方程的推断。
